medical_history.history_of_present_illness: 46,XX SRY-negative karyotypes, concurrent referral with severe scrotal congenital hypospadias, later developed gynecomastia and signs of inchoate puberty with axillary and genital body hair growth
medical_history.past_medical_history: Hypergonadotropic hypogonadism with low free and total serum testosterone and elevated levels of sex hormone-binding globulin (SHBG), follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH), luteinizing hormone (LH) and normal, but marginally increased, estradiol
medical_history.family_history: Father had duplications upstream of SOX9, father's sister had severe psychological and alcohol abuse problems, committed completed suicide at the age of 26 years

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Abdomino-pelvico-scrotal ultrasonography and magnetic resonance imaging revealed a cystic ovotestis on the right side and an atrophic testis on the left side of the scrotum and a normal prostate of appropriate size and localization

laboratory_examinations.procedure_name: Giemsa-banding and FISH with LSI SRY/CEP X probes

laboratory_examinations.findings: Duplications upstream of SOX9 with sizes, distance from transcriptional start site and breakpoints: patient 1: 153.00 kbp, 449.16–602.16 kbp, chr17: 69,515,001–69,668,000 bp; patient 2: 155.13 kbp, 450.23–605.36 kbp, chr17: 69,511,806–69,666,935 bp; father: 143.00 kbp, 462.12–605.36 kbp, chr17: 69,511,806–69,655,045 bp. Mother’s examination results were normal.

Patient 1: hypergonadotropic hypogonadism with low free and total serum testosterone and elevated levels of SHBG, FSH, LH and normal, but marginally increased, estradiol. Before the age of 14, he had normal male prepubertal hormone levels. Patient 2: normal male FSH, LH, and SHBG levels, and normal, but marginally increased, serum estradiol and normal, but marginally low, free and total testosterone levels.
laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Patient 1 showed progression from normal prepubertal hormone levels to hypergonadotropic hypogonadism by age 14.

radiographic_examinations.procedure_name: Abdomino-pelvico-scrotal ultrasonography and magnetic resonance imaging
radiographic_examinations.findings: Patient 1: cystic ovotestis (3.7 × 2.8 × 2.3 cm) on the right side and an atrophic testis (0.6 × 1.3 × 1.5 cm) on the left side of the scrotum and a normal prostate of appropriate size (2.9 × 1.8 × 2.3 cm) and localization. Patient 2: one scrotal and one inguinal ovotestis and a tubular structure (5.5 × 2.3 cm) behind the bladder assessed to be a Müllerian duct residue (a closed vagina).

other_tests.findings: Patient 1: a few, mainly closed, tubuli seminiferi contorti with immature Sertoli cells. Occasionally, germinal cells were observable without any signs of maturity. The basal membranes of the tubules were thick, and the interstitium included very few Leydig cells with irregular, eosinophilic, rarely vacuolated cytoplasms. Within the sparse connective tissue, only a few primordial follicles were visible next to some ovarian stroma. Patient 2: numerous primordial follicles and a few cystous areas between ovarian spindle cells characteristically structured into plexuses, and some closed testicular tubules.
